title: spermacoce asperula
intro: Spermacoce asperula, also known as the Woodruff or Wild Baby's Breath, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Spermacoce asperula include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Spermacoce asperula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
